specific_location: "Metro Lake" on Graneros Blvd about 1 mile west of exit 71, I-25
description: Size: medium size Gull about 3/4 size of nearby Mallards.  Head: smokey off-white with prominent black smudge behind eye. At most angles off-white nape did not contrast with rest of the head and face. But in certain light a pale dappling of gray appreared on nape, though dappling was difficult to see.  Body & wings: At rest, back was solid dark gray becoming a bit lighter in the wing coverts. Primaries were solid black, long, and extended well beyond tail. In flight the gull also appeared fairly long winged with dark gray mantle melding into darker black primaries. Rump and tail were white with narrow but prominent black terminal tail band appearing to extend to outer rectrices. Underparts were smokey white and showed no discernible contrasts with off-white portions of head.  Bare parts: Bill was solid black, slightly downcurved at tip, and long, appearing as long as head length measured from nape to lores. Eye was solid dark. Legs and feet were solid black.Behaviors: Bird was first spotted swimming with nearby Mallards. Flew to shoreline were it often stood motionless or walked about a few yardsCall: none givenPlumage: first summer
